violations.claim: Information flow + state machine: log at line 40 is the first statement.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: [NEW]
; LINE: 40
; Information flow + state machine: log at line 40 is the first statement.
; It fires before any validation (none exists). It records "cancelled"
; as a fait accompli even when VALID_TRANSITIONS forbids the transition.
; A shipped order (pre_state=3) produces a false audit entry.

(declare-const pre_state Int)
(declare-const log_fires Int)        ; 1 = log statement executes
(declare-const transition_valid Int) ; 1 = transition permitted by VALID_TRANSITIONS

; Code transition: log is the first statement — always executes (no early return before it)
(assert (= log_fires 1))

; VALID_TRANSITIONS encodes which pre-states permit cancellation
(assert (=> (= pre_state 0) (= transition_valid 1)))
(assert (=> (= pre_state 1) (= transition_valid 1)))
(assert (=> (= pre_state 2) (= transition_valid 1)))
(assert (=> (= pre_state 3) (= transition_valid 0)))  ; shipped: no valid transitions
(assert (=> (= pre_state 4) (= transition_valid 0)))  ; cancelled: no valid transitions

; Violation: log fires claiming cancellation when the transition is forbidden
(assert (= log_fires 1))
(assert (= transition_valid 0))

(check-sat)

clause_history.clause:
; PRINCIPLE: [NEW]
; LINE: 40
; Information flow + state machine: log at line 40 is the first statement.
; It fires before any validation (none exists). It records "cancelled"
; as a fait accompli even when VALID_TRANSITIONS forbids the transition.
; A shipped order (pre_state=3) produces a false audit entry.

(declare-const pre_state Int)
(declare-const log_fires Int)        ; 1 = log statement executes
(declare-const transition_valid Int) ; 1 = transition permitted by VALID_TRANSITIONS

; Code transition: log is the first statement — always executes (no early return before it)
(assert (= log_fires 1))

; VALID_TRANSITIONS encodes which pre-states permit cancellation
(assert (=> (= pre_state 0) (= transition_valid 1)))
(assert (=> (= pre_state 1) (= transition_valid 1)))
(assert (=> (= pre_state 2) (= transition_valid 1)))
(assert (=> (= pre_state 3) (= transition_valid 0)))  ; shipped: no valid transitions
(assert (=> (= pre_state 4) (= transition_valid 0)))  ; cancelled: no valid transitions

; Violation: log fires claiming cancellation when the transition is forbidden
(assert (= log_fires 1))
(assert (= transition_valid 0))

(check-sat)
